title: triticum ovatum subsp. biunciale
intro: Triticum ovatum subsp. biunciale is a lesser-known subspecies of the wild wheat plant, characterized by its unique biuncial spikelet. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been valued for its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Triticum ovatum subsp. biunciale can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies to promote overall well-being.
